name: GraphQL
description: Base module for integrating GraphQL with Drupal; exposes your Drupal data model through configurable GraphQL schemas and endpoints.
library_dependencies: webonyx/graphql-php (^15.32.3) — the underlying PHP GraphQL server/executor
provides_config_entities: graphql_server (config_prefix graphql_servers; each entity = one schema bound to one HTTP endpoint path)
provides_route_defaults: _graphql (marks a route as a GraphQL endpoint; RouteProvider mints graphql.query.<server_id> per graphql_server entity from its endpoint path)
provides_base_classes: Drupal\graphql\Plugin\GraphQL\SchemaExtension\SdlSchemaExtensionPluginBase (adds SDL + resolvers to a composable schema)
ecosystem_note: Developer FRAMEWORK for building GraphQL servers, not a turnkey feature. Depends on typed_data and bundles webonyx/graphql-php as the executor. The central object is the graphql_server config entity (config_prefix graphql_servers): each one names a Schema plugin, holds that schema's schema_configuration, and declares an HTTP endpoint path; RouteProvider then registers a POST/GET JSON route graphql.query.<id> at that path (controller RequestController::handleRequest). Building a schema means writing a Schema plugin (or, more commonly, using the ComposableSchema plus one or more SchemaExtension plugins) that pairs SDL (.graphqls type definitions) with resolvers wired via the ResolverBuilder/ResolverRegistry, where each field pulls data through DataProducer plugins (small reusable resolution steps). Introspection is on by default and there is an in-browser Explorer/Voyager at /admin/config/graphql. A bare install ships the plugin types + the server config entity but NO servers and NO usable schema — you either write one or enable the graphql_composable example submodule (schema id composable_example) for a working endpoint. This is the successor to the 3.x/4.x line; 4.x/5.x share the same architecture (config entity + SDL schema + data producers).
